              <msub id="msub_24">
                <mrow>
                  <mi id="mi_168">Ω</mi>
                </mrow>
                <mrow>
                  <mi id="mi_169">N</mi>
                </mrow>
              </msub>
            </mstyle>

<mstyle id="custom-group-3" class="custom-group"><msup id="msup_7">
            <mrow>
              <mi id="mi_145">H</mi>
            </mrow>

              <mn>1</mn>

          </msup>
          <mrow>
            <mo class="bigl" fence="true">(</mo>
            <mrow>
              <mi mathvariant="script" id="mi_146">T</mi>
              <mo>;</mo>
              <mstyle id="custom-group-2" class="custom-group"><msubsup id="msubsup_4">
                <mrow>
                  <mi id="mi_147">H</mi>
                </mrow>
                <mrow>

concepts_list.name: u
concepts_list.description: The displacement field of the wave equation
concepts_list.justification: Explicitly defined as the dependent variable in the wave equation and appears in multiple forms such as u(t, x) and its derivatives.

concepts_list.name: w
concepts_list.description: The test function used in the weak formulation of the wave equation
concepts_list.justification: Appears repeatedly as a test function in the weak form equations (7) and (8), and is required to belong to the Sobolev space H^1_0(Ω).

concepts_list.name: rho
concepts_list.description: Mass per unit volume of the medium
concepts_list.justification: Defined as the positive mass per unit volume in the wave equation and used in the time derivative term of the equation.

concepts_list.name: T
concepts_list.description: Tensile pre-stress as load per unit surface area
concepts_list.justification: Defined as the positive tensile pre-stress in the wave equation and appears in the stress term involving the gradient of displacement.

concepts_list.name: Omega
concepts_list.description: The main spatial computational domain in d-dimensional space
concepts_list.justification: Defined as a d-dimensional spatial domain Ω ⊂ R^d and appears in multiple contexts including integration domains and subdomain partitions.

concepts_list.name: Gamma
concepts_list.description: The collection of interfaces between subdomains
concepts_list.justification: Defined as the interface between subdomains Ω_i and appears in transmission conditions (5a) and (5b).

concepts_list.name: t
concepts_list.justification: Used as the time variable in the wave equation and appears in time derivatives and in the temporal domain T = [0, t_end].

concepts_list.name: H_0_1_Omega
concepts_list.description: Sobolev space of functions with zero boundary conditions and first-order derivatives
concepts_list.justification: Defined as H^1_0(Ω) and used as the space for the test functions and solution in the weak formulation.

concepts_list.name: H_1
concepts_list.description: Space of functions with continuous first derivatives in time and spatial Sobolev space H^1_0(Ω)
concepts_list.justification: Defined as H^1(T; H^1_0(Ω)) and used to describe the regularity of the solution in the temporal domain.

concepts_list.name: H_minus1
concepts_list.description: Dual space of H^1_0(Ω) used for duality pairing
concepts_list.justification: Defined as H^{-1}(Ω) and used in the duality pairing between the time derivative term and the test function space.

concepts_list.name: d_Omega
concepts_list.description: Volume element of integration over the domain Ω
concepts_list.justification: Appears in integrals over Ω such as ∫_Ω ρ ∂²u/∂t² w dΩ and ∫_Ω T ∇u ⋅ ∇w dΩ.

concepts_list.name: d_Gamma
concepts_list.description: Surface element of integration over the boundary Γ
concepts_list.justification: Appears in integrals over Γ such as ∫_Γ [T ∂n u] w dΓ and ∫_∂Ω g w dΓ.

concepts_list.name: g
concepts_list.description: Neumann boundary condition on the boundary ∂Ω_N
concepts_list.justification: Defined as the boundary condition on ∂Ω_N in equation (4b) and appears in the weak form as a source term.

concepts_list.name: cal_T
concepts_list.description: Temporal domain of integration from 0 to t_end
concepts_list.justification: Defined as the script T = [0, t_end] and used in the weak formulation to specify the time interval over which the solution is defined.

new_concept_info.description:
The displacement field of the wave equation
JUSTIFICATION:
Explicitly defined as the dependent variable in the wave equation and appears in multiple forms such as u(t, x) and its derivatives.

The test function used in the weak formulation of the wave equation
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears repeatedly as a test function in the weak form equations (7) and (8), and is required to belong to the Sobolev space H^1_0(Ω).

Mass per unit volume of the medium
JUSTIFICATION:
Defined as the positive mass per unit volume in the wave equation and used in the time derivative term of the equation.

Tensile pre-stress as load per unit surface area
JUSTIFICATION:
Defined as the positive tensile pre-stress in the wave equation and appears in the stress term involving the gradient of displacement.

The main spatial computational domain in d-dimensional space
JUSTIFICATION:
Defined as a d-dimensional spatial domain Ω ⊂ R^d and appears in multiple contexts including integration domains and subdomain partitions.

The collection of interfaces between subdomains
JUSTIFICATION:
Defined as the interface between subdomains Ω_i and appears in transmission conditions (5a) and (5b).

Temporal variable representing time
JUSTIFICATION:
Used as the time variable in the wave equation and appears in time derivatives and in the temporal domain T = [0, t_end].

Space of functions with continuous first derivatives in time and spatial Sobolev space H^1_0(Ω)
JUSTIFICATION:
Defined as H^1(T; H^1_0(Ω)) and used to describe the regularity of the solution in the temporal domain.

Dual space of H^1_0(Ω) used for duality pairing
JUSTIFICATION:
Defined as H^{-1}(Ω) and used in the duality pairing between the time derivative term and the test function space.

Volume element of integration over the domain Ω
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears in integrals over Ω such as ∫_Ω ρ ∂²u/∂t² w dΩ and ∫_Ω T ∇u ⋅ ∇w dΩ.

Surface element of integration over the boundary Γ
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears in integrals over Γ such as ∫_Γ [T ∂n u] w dΓ and ∫_∂Ω g w dΓ.

Neumann boundary condition on the boundary ∂Ω_N
JUSTIFICATION:
Defined as the boundary condition on ∂Ω_N in equation (4b) and appears in the weak form as a source term.

Temporal domain of integration from 0 to t_end
JUSTIFICATION:
Defined as the script T = [0, t_end] and used in the weak formulation to specify the time interval over which the solution is defined.
